hero_subtitle: SFTY connects wallet, transaction, and counterparty data into a unified workflow that helps compliance teams assess sanctions risk with traceable evidence and structured cases.

trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk

problem_title: Counterparty sanctions exposure lacks clear linkage
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to assess sanctions risks when wallet activity, transaction monitoring, and counterparty data remain siloed across multiple systems. This fragmentation slows risk detection, complicates evidence tracking, and weakens audit readiness during sanctions reviews.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects wallet activity, transaction risks, and counterparty profiles into one workflow, enabling compliance teams to assess sanctions exposure with clear evidence, audit trails, and streamlined case management.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to sanctions case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY links wallet and transaction risk signals to sanctions screening alerts and escalates them into structured cases with documented evidence for review.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond wallet alerts. Connected sanctions evidence
why_sfty_description: SFTY links sanctions exposure signals to wallet and transaction data, enabling compliance teams to manage risk through cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY gives VASP compliance teams a controlled layer to aggregate provider outputs, preserve audit evidence and support bank due diligence in enterprise-grade deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how to assess counterparty sanctions exposure
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how transaction risk links to sanctions exposure cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports sanctions exposure reviews

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY support sanctions exposure monitoring?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects wallet and transaction data with sanctions screening outputs to help compliance teams identify and manage counterparty sanctions exposure in one structured workflow.
faq_2_question: How are transaction and wallet risks aggregated?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates risk signals from multiple blockchain analytics and KYT providers, linking wallets, transactions and counterparties to build a comprehensive sanctions risk profile.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready compliance evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and supporting evidence in auditable records to support review readiness and defensible compliance oversight.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with your existing KYC, KYT, sanctions screening and Travel Rule providers, structuring their outputs into connected compliance workflows and cases.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace sanctions providers but connects their outputs into case management, evidence packages and reporting workflows for operational use.
final_title: Connect sanctions exposure into structured case workflows
final_description: See how SFTY links wallet, transaction and counterparty data into controlled workflows for sanctions risk review and audit evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
